title: Scrapy Architecture, Scheduler, Middleware, AutoThrottle, and Observability · Behavior Case: Crawl Observability
summary: A hand-built crawler grows hidden hooks, middleware order changes behavior, duplicate filters lose identity, AutoThrottle is treated as permission, and metrics cannot reconstruct failures. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawl observability increment in Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan. Learners progress…
objectives: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction.
Configure latency target, concurrency, delay bounds, errors, robots and owner limits and treat AutoThrottle as load control, not authorization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example site-quality team handles case 7361 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction. A defensible solution applies Crawl Observability, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to Scrapy 2.17 scale and observability transfer plan.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-scrapy-scale-observability-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-scrapy-scale-observability-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_scrapy_scale_observability_5(component_boundaries: int = 8, verified_boundaries: int = 8, middleware_orders: int = 6, metric_families: int = 10, hidden_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if component_boundaries < 6 or verified_boundaries < 0:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "scrapy-architecture"
    if verified_boundaries != component_boundaries:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "component-reconciliation"
    if middleware_orders < 4 or metric_families < 8:
        return False, metric_families, "scale-evidence"
    if hidden_failures > 0:
        return False, hidden_failures, "observability-gap"
    assert verified_boundaries == component_boundaries and hidden_failures == 0
    return True, metric_families, "observable-scrapy-transfer"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-crawl-observability-dgdth8
# Competency: scraper-crawl-observability.
# Case 9692: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_crawl_observability_dgdth8(component_boundaries: int = 8, verified_boundaries: int = 8, middleware_orders: int = 6, metric_families: int = 10, hidden_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dgdth8 = "dgdth8-8htdgd-9692"
    assert evidence_variant_dgdth8
    evidence_axes_dgdth8 = (
        "authorizationdgdth8",
        "url9692",
        "response8htdgd",
        "treedgdth89692",
        "frontier9692dgdth8",
        "report8htdgd9692",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dgdth8)) == 6
    if component_boundaries < 6 or verified_boundaries < 0:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "scrapy-architecture"
    if verified_boundaries != component_boundaries:
        return False, verified_boundaries, "component-reconciliation"
    if middleware_orders < 4 or metric_families < 8:
        return False, metric_families, "scale-evidence"
    if hidden_failures > 0:
        return False, hidden_failures, "observability-gap"
    assert verified_boundaries == component_boundaries and hidden_failures == 0
    return True, metric_families, "observable-scrapy-transfer"

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Scrapy 2.17.0 Documentation — Engine, scheduler, downloader, spiders, pipelines, middleware, robots, AutoThrottle, feeds, persistence, signals, statistics, and operations.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Repair the Crawl Observability defect · Theory Crawl Observability, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Crawl Observability precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Repair the Crawl Observability defect · Theory Crawl Observability, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 1078 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction.

For Authorized crawling and Python Repair the Crawl Observability defect · Theory Crawl Observability, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Aggregate request and item counts are sufficient production diagnostics.

stimulus.title: Crawl Observability failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Aggregate request and item counts are sufficient production diagnostics.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the crawl observability defect evidence boundary.

For repair the crawl observability defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Crawl Observability, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Crawl Observability model · Theory Crawl Observability
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Crawl Observability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 2697 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to map the crawler into Scrapy architecture, scheduler, middleware, AutoThrottle, and reconstructable telemetry; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Crawl Observability model · Theory Crawl Observability, keep Crawl Observability connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Correlate crawl, request, response, page, extraction, queue, retry, block, privacy, output, and recovery events with bounded labels and redaction.
